input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule EV.6.7.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.6.7.3
            TITLE: electrical conductivity of any part may be tested by checking any point which is likely to be

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.7.3: Electrical conductivity of any part may be tested by checking any point which is likely to be
EV.6.7.3 Electrical conductivity of any part may be tested by checking any point which is likely to be 
conductive.   
Where no convenient conductive point is available, an area of coating may be removed.

final_answer: Electrical conductivity of any part may be tested by checking any point which is likely to be conductive. Where no convenient conductive point is available, an area of coating may be removed.
